title: node-postgres Pools, Queries, Transactions, and Failure Semantics · Type Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Create one bounded pg Pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior.
Use parameterized values, allowlisted identifiers, explicit columns, row validation, cardinality checks, timeouts, and cancellation without SQL string concatenation.
Acquire and release a client exactly once with finally while distinguishing acquisition, query, protocol, server, timeout, cancellation, and application failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Order the regression proof · Debug Pg Ambiguous Commit
steps.instruction:
Order the Pg Ambiguous Commit Node and Express server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

content.text: A debug team handles case 6441 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Pg Ambiguous Commit has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Pg Ambiguous Commit, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A connection error during COMMIT proves the transaction rolled back and is safe to retry.
